verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the β-subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase Complex V, a core catalytic subunit of the F1 head domain. The protein is synthesized on mitochondrial ribosomes, remains within the mitochondrial matrix, and is integrated into the inner mitochondrial membrane as part of the membrane-spanning Fo-F1 complex. No documented surface display, PM cycling, lysosomal exocytosis, cell-state-induced translocation, tissue-restricted surface pool, or covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. Not a secreted protein, not subject to ectodomain shedding, and no known cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool. The protein is exclusively mitochondrial-internal.
